statistical_overview.complexity_variance: HIGH - Commands range from 174 to 442 lines
statistical_overview.savage_comment: These commands are more inflated than a 1980s hair band. The average command could be a short story.

evidence: 309 lines to say 'plan your morning' - War and Peace was shorter
12 thinking blocks for something humans do naturally
Uses more MCP calls than a Fortune 500 helpdesk
Has 25% bloat ratio with XML theater
Solves a $5 problem with a $5000 solution

evidence: 442 lines to improve code - The cure is worse than the disease
18 thinking blocks means 18 ways to overthink simple tasks
35% of content is XML theater with no functional value

Promises 'intelligent' enhancement but delivers bureaucratic bloat
savage_assessment.reality_check: Good developers enhance code by... enhancing code. Not by writing novels about it.

statistical_comparison.maintenance_cost: EXTREME - More complex than the code it enhances

evidence: 415 lines to add reasoning - The irony of unreasonable reasoning tools
45% XML bloat ratio - More XML than actual logic
Can 'enhance itself' - Self-referential nightmare fuel

most_bloated.comment: Achieves the rare feat of making reasoning unreasonable

least_efficient.comment: Uses 207x more tokens than necessary - A masterclass in inefficiency

highest_failure_rate.comment: 69% chance of failure - Worse odds than a lottery ticket

most_over_engineered.comment: 89x more complex than needed - Engineering achievement in wrong direction

bloat_distribution.interpretation: Consistently high bloat across all commands - This is systematic over-engineering

token_efficiency.interpretation: Universally terrible efficiency - All commands waste 88%+ of their tokens

complexity_correlation.interpretation: Strong NEGATIVE correlation - More lines = Less value. This is anti-engineering.

failure_probability.interpretation: Average 52% failure rate - You'd get better odds flipping a coin

development_cost.opportunity_cost: Could have built actual useful features instead

roi_calculation.verdict: MASSIVE NEGATIVE ROI - These commands destroy value

immediate_actions: DELETE 80% of XML bloat - Keep only functional content
MERGE similar thinking blocks - 16 blocks for reasoning is insane
REMOVE MCP dependencies where unnecessary - Not everything needs memory
SIMPLIFY language - Senior developers prefer clarity over verbosity
ADD actual examples - Stop describing, start demonstrating
architectural_fixes: Use functions instead of XML templates
Make commands do ONE thing well instead of everything poorly
Remove self-referential complexity - No command should enhance itself
Focus on user outcomes, not internal processes
performance_improvements: Target 95% token reduction across all commands
Aim for <20 line commands for simple tasks
Use direct action instead of thinking about thinking

evidence_summary: Average 82% bloat rate - Like adding concrete to a helicopter
5.6x worse token efficiency than random text generation
52% average failure rate - Worse than random chance
359 average lines per command - Novellas disguised as tools
Negative ROI of 89.4% - Actively destroying value
savage_final_verdict.root_cause_analysis: Classic engineer's disease: Solving simple problems with complex solutions because you can, not because you should
savage_final_verdict.prescription: Immediate simplification therapy. Remove 90% of content. Focus on outcomes, not processes. Remember: The best code is no code.
savage_final_verdict.prognosis: Without intervention, these commands will collapse under their own complexity weight within 6 months
